prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetie, a CPU is like the little brain inside the computer. It helps the machine think and figure out what to do when you type, click, or play a game. I imagine it like a busy postmaster, sorting letters and sending them to the right places so everything gets done in order. It’s a tiny chip tucked inside the computer, but it works very hard all day long. Even though I don’t quite understand how it fits in there, I know it’s important—kind of like how your brain tells your hands to tie your shoes. Without the CPU, the computer wouldn’t know how to listen to you or do what you ask.
